title: Assets, Media Admission, Fingerprints, CSS, and Integrity · Runtime Lens: Media Performance
summary: Assets are copied by basename, path traversal escapes the output root, CSS references are missed, hashes change with timestamps, huge images ship unchanged, and stale files survive rebuilds. Predict the reader decision, source identity, parse state, output URL, accessibility result, failure, and evidence before reading the governing source. Planned artifact: Add and verify the media performance increment in bounded content-addressed asset manifest. Learners progress through prediction, explicit…
objectives: Generate or admit responsive image variants, dimensions, formats, loading hints, fonts, and fallbacks against quality and byte budgets.
Resolve and rewrite document, template, CSS, source-map, icon, manifest, and responsive-media references through one asset manifest.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""build-static-site-python cumulative production static-site generator portfolio.

Browser execution uses deterministic pure-Python parser, route, render, graph, and publication models
with original fixed in-memory fixtures only. Native filesystems, full CommonMark, markdown-it-py,
Jinja, validators, browsers, GitHub Actions, Pages, DNS, TLS, CDN caches, and production effects
require controlled transfer gates.
"""

from dataclasses import dataclass


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class PublicationPolicy:
    base_path: str = "/handbook/"
    encoding: str = "utf-8"
    raw_html_allowed: bool = False
    max_document_bytes: int = 262_144
    max_nesting_depth: int = 64


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class BuildEvidence:
    revision: str
    content_manifest_digest: str
    route_manifest_digest: str
    artifact_digest: str
    accessibility_review_id: str


def portfolio_ready() -> str:
    return "Python static-site generator portfolio ready"

content.title: Worked case pattern
content.text: A worked example publishing team handles case 6607 during a project-subpath GitHub Pages deployment. Build deterministic Python evidence to admit, fingerprint, rewrite, and reconcile bounded licensed assets; keep browser work pure, deterministic, bounded, and free of native filesystem, template engine, validator, network, GitHub, DNS, TLS, CDN, and host effects; then publish below a project subpath and verify every internal URL, fragment, asset, feed, and canonical. Browser code uses pure bounded models and original fixed fixtures only. Real filesystems, full CommonMark, markdown-it-py, Jinja, validators, browsers, GitHub Actions, Pages, DNS, TLS, CDN caches, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Generate or admit responsive image variants, dimensions, formats, loading hints, fonts, and fallbacks against quality and byte budgets. A defensible solution applies Media Performance, preserves the governing invariant or value contract, and connects the observed result to bounded content-addressed asset manifest.

content.code:
# Evidence: ssg-worked-static-site-assets-fingerprints-2
# Competency: ssg-worked-static-site-assets-fingerprints-2.
# Case 7417: a project-subpath GitHub Pages deployment.
# Operating constraint: record the first failed invariant, repair, regression evidence, reader task, rollback, recovery, and residual-risk owner.
# Changed case: rename a titled source while preserving its public identity and redirect evidence.
def worked_static_site_assets_fingerprints_2(assets: int = 16, admitted: int = 14, rejected: int = 2, missing_rewrites: int = 0, digest_collisions: int = 0) -> tuple[bool, int | float, str]:
    evidence_variant_worked2 = "worked2-2dekrow-7417"
    assert evidence_variant_worked2
    evidence_axes_worked2 = (
        "readerworked2",
        "source7417",
        "parse2dekrow",
        "routeworked27417",
        "artifact7417worked2",
        "recovery2dekrow7417",
    )
    assert len(set(evidence_axes_worked2)) == 6
    if min(assets, admitted, rejected) < 0:
        return False, assets, "asset-count"
    if admitted + rejected != assets:
        return False, admitted, "asset-reconciliation"
    if missing_rewrites > 0:
        return False, missing_rewrites, "asset-reference"
    if digest_collisions > 0:
        return False, digest_collisions, "asset-collision"
    assert admitted + rejected == assets
    return True, admitted, "fingerprinted-assets"

content.code:
# Evidence: ssg-media-performance-chqfyx
# Competency: ssg-media-performance.
# Case 2336: a large technical reference incremental rebuild.
# Operating constraint: test empty, malformed, deeply nested, Unicode, collision, unsafe URL, missing asset, stale cache, rejected, and recovery cases.
# Changed case: delete a source and change a shared layout, then compare incremental and clean artifact digests.
def evidence_ssg_media_performance_chqfyx(assets: int = 16, admitted: int = 14, rejected: int = 2, missing_rewrites: int = 0, digest_collisions: int = 0) -> tuple[bool, int | float, str]:
    evidence_variant_chqfyx = "chqfyx-xyfqhc-2336"
    assert evidence_variant_chqfyx
    evidence_axes_chqfyx = (
        "readerchqfyx",
        "source2336",
        "parsexyfqhc",
        "routechqfyx2336",
        "artifact2336chqfyx",
        "recoveryxyfqhc2336",
    )
    assert len(set(evidence_axes_chqfyx)) == 6
    if min(assets, admitted, rejected) < 0:
        return False, assets, "asset-count"
    if admitted + rejected != assets:
        return False, admitted, "asset-reconciliation"
    if missing_rewrites > 0:
        return False, missing_rewrites, "asset-reference"
    if digest_collisions > 0:
        return False, digest_collisions, "asset-collision"
    assert admitted + rejected == assets
    return True, admitted, "fingerprinted-assets"

steps.instruction:
Write the theory evidence note for Media Performance: explain the governing condition, one failure, and how this concrete case was verified — A theory publishing team handles case 4151 during an accessibility-led community newsroom release. Build deterministic Python evidence to admit, fingerprint, rewrite, and reconcile bounded licensed assets; test empty, malformed, deeply nested, Unicode, collision, unsafe URL, missing asset, stale cache, rejected, and recovery cases; then insert malformed nested delimiters and retain later valid content with a located diagnostic. Browser code uses pure bounded models and original fixed fixtures only. Real filesystems, full CommonMark, markdown-it-py, Jinja, validators, browsers, GitHub Actions, Pages, DNS, TLS, CDN caches, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Generate or admit responsive image variants, dimensions, formats, loading hints, fonts, and fallbacks against quality and byte budgets.

Evidence target: Static-site publishing and Python Defend the Media Performance model · Theory Media Performance.
steps.why: Retrieval and explanation expose gaps that recognition questions can hide. For Static-site publishing and Python Defend the Media Performance model · Theory Media Performance, keep Media Performance connected to executable Python source, parse, route, render, accessibility, artifact, deployment, and recovery decision evidence.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Generate or admit responsive image variants, dimensions, formats, loading hints, fonts, and fallbacks against quality and byte budgets.

hints: For Static-site publishing and Python Defend the Media Performance model · Theory Media Performance, name the reader, content authority, revision, source, span, parser, trust, route, render, asset, accessibility, artifact, deployment, and recovery boundary, Media Performance precondition, or invariant that must hold.

At Static-site publishing and Python Defend the Media Performance model · Theory Media Performance, trace this concrete input and predict admission, decoding, tokenization, parsing, transformation, routing, rendering, validation, publication, failure, rollback, and recovery evidence before reviewing it: A theory publishing team handles case 4151 during an accessibility-led community newsroom release. Build deterministic Python evidence to admit, fingerprint, rewrite, and reconcile bounded licensed assets; test empty, malformed, deeply nested, Unicode, collision, unsafe URL, missing asset, stale cache, rejected, and recovery cases; then insert malformed nested delimiters and retain later valid content with a located diagnostic. Browser code uses pure bounded models and original fixed fixtures only. Real filesystems, full CommonMark, markdown-it-py, Jinja, validators, browsers, GitHub Actions, Pages, DNS, TLS, CDN caches, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Generate or admit responsive image variants, dimensions, formats, loading hints, fonts, and fallbacks against quality and byte budgets.

For Static-site publishing and Python Defend the Media Performance model · Theory Media Performance, challenge the documented misconception with changed-source, delimiter, Unicode, nesting, route, URL, asset, viewport, cache, security, deployment, recovery, and controlled production-transfer evidence: Converting every image to the newest format is automatically faster and accessible.

checks.description: The cumulative python portfolio contains this competency’s named evidence increment.
checks.failureMessage: Add the exact evidence marker # Evidence: ssg-media-performance-chqfyx.

checks.pattern: # Evidence: ssg-media-performance-chqfyx(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?def\s+evidence_ssg_media_performance_chqfyx\s*\((?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?\)\s*(?:->[^:]+)?\s*:(?=(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?min\(assets, admitted, rejected\) < 0)(?=(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?admitted \+ rejected != assets)(?=(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?missing_rewrites > 0)(?=(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?digest_collisions > 0)(?=(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?assert)(?=(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?return)(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?return
checks.description: The increment includes the required executable or reviewable evidence structure.
